# AI SDK Kotlin

AI SDK Kotlin is a Kotlin Multiplatform, green-room port of Vercel AI SDK v6
public feature areas. It is not an official Vercel package and not a
TypeScript source translation.

Repository: https://github.com/torad-labs/aisdk-kotlin
Group/artifact: ai.torad:torad-aisdk
Current version: 0.3.0-beta01
Primary package: ai.torad.aisdk

## What To Read First

- README.md: install, targets, included features, and quick start.
- docs/wiki/README.md: human docs navigation.
- docs/wiki/foundations.md: mental model, Kotlin differences, and reading
  paths.
- docs/wiki/getting-started.md: first agent and provider choices.
- docs/wiki/core.md: provider-neutral generation, settings, results, and
  errors.
- docs/wiki/prompts-and-messages.md: prompt shapes, ModelMessage,
  ContentPart, tool results, approval responses, and UI conversion.
- docs/wiki/prompt-engineering.md: system prompts, task prompts, retrieval
  context, tool descriptions, and structured-output prompting.
- docs/wiki/settings-and-provider-options.md: sampling, limits, cancellation,
  provider-specific options, defaults, and dynamic agent settings.
- docs/wiki/structured-output.md: Output.obj, arrays, choices, JSON trees,
  streaming typed data, and object helper migration.
- docs/wiki/tools.md: typed tools, streaming tools, approval, toModelOutput,
  dynamic tools, provider-executed tools, and input hooks.
- docs/wiki/streaming.md: Flow<StreamEvent>, streamResult, adapters,
  server responses, cancellation, tool streams, and errors.
- docs/wiki/advanced-streaming.md: stopping, resume tradeoffs, backpressure,
  fanout, replay, caching, and cleanup.
- docs/wiki/model-families.md: embeddings, reranking, image, speech,
  transcription, video, and generated files.
- docs/wiki/middleware-and-telemetry.md: language model middleware, default
  settings, JSON extraction, simulated streaming, telemetry, and DevTools.
- docs/wiki/error-handling.md: typed SDK errors, retry policy, streaming
  failures, tool errors, and UI validation.
- docs/wiki/lifecycle-and-events.md: agent hooks, stream events, tool input
  hooks, telemetry events, and UI finish handling.
- docs/wiki/agents.md: Agent, ToolLoopAgent, stop conditions, lifecycle hooks,
  tool approval, subagents, and memory boundaries.
- docs/wiki/providers.md: gateway, OpenAI-compatible, dedicated facades,
  provider registry, and custom providers.
- docs/wiki/provider-management.md: Gateway metadata, provider routing,
  registries, middleware, and custom providers.
- docs/wiki/ui-and-streams.md: Flow<StreamEvent>, UIMessage, Chat, transports,
  stream responses, and persistence.
- docs/wiki/ui-stream-protocols.md: text streams, UI message streams, host
  writers, custom UI streams, ids, metadata, and finish handling.
- docs/wiki/chatbots.md: DirectChatTransport, ChatSession, persistence, tool
  rendering, approvals, resume, and text-only transports.
- docs/wiki/completion-and-object-ui.md: completion state, completion facades,
  partial structured object state, and object facades.
- docs/wiki/framework-facades.md: framework-neutral UI messages, chat and
  agent sessions, stream responses, and host-framework integration boundaries.
- docs/wiki/mcp.md: MCP transports, tools, resources, prompts, elicitation,
  and cleanup.
- docs/wiki/application-patterns.md: tool organization, agent composition,
  call configuration, approval, UI dispatch, and subagents.
- docs/wiki/workflow-patterns.md: sequential calls, routing, parallel work,
  evaluator-optimizer loops, agents as orchestrators, and manual loops.
- docs/wiki/memory.md: conversation memory, summaries, retrieval memory,
  memory tools, and context injection.
- docs/wiki/cookbook.md: short examples for common SDK tasks.
- docs/wiki/devtools.md: recorder-backed local inspection for generate and
  stream calls.
- docs/wiki/utilities.md: ids, stream smoothing, simulated streams, message
  pruning, embeddings math, abort signals, and data URLs.
- docs/wiki/coding-agents.md: source-of-truth map and commands for coding
  agents.
- docs/wiki/testing-and-release.md: local checks, CI, parity, and release.
- docs/wiki/troubleshooting.md: common failures.
- docs/parity/README.md: generated Vercel AI SDK v6 package/export parity
  ledger.
- docs/AISDK_PORT.md: Kotlin mapping for Vercel AI SDK v6 concepts.
- docs/KOTLIN_SDK_BEST_PRACTICES.md: engineering standard for KMP SDK work.

## Core Runtime Concepts

- Agent<TContext, TOutput>: provider-neutral multi-step generation contract.
- ToolLoopAgent: default tool-loop implementation.
- LanguageModel, EmbeddingModel, ImageModel, SpeechModel, TranscriptionModel,
  VideoModel, RerankingModel: provider-neutral model contracts.
- TextGenerator.generate, TextGenerator.stream, and TextGenerator.streamResult:
  high-level text APIs over GenerationInput and CallConfig.
- Output and StructuredObjectGenerator: structured output adapters for Kotlin
  serializers and schemas.
- Tool, DynamicTool, StreamingTool, ProviderExecutedTool, and ToolSet: typed,
  dynamic, streaming, provider-executed, and grouped tool definitions.
- StreamEvent: low-level streaming event taxonomy.
- UIMessage and UIMessagePart: framework-neutral render contracts.
- Chat and ChatTransport: host-neutral chat state and transport contracts.
- Gateway, OpenAICompatible, OpenAI, and provider facades: provider entry
  points.
- ProviderRegistry.createProviderRegistry and CustomProvider: provider routing.
- CreateMCPClient, MCPClientConfig, and CreateMcpTransport: MCP client and
  transport entry points.
- DevToolsMiddleware: recorder-backed development middleware.

## Development Commands

```sh
node tools/check-ai-sdk-reference.mjs
node tools/generate-parity-ledger.mjs --check
./gradlew jvmTest
./gradlew check publishToMavenLocal
```

Use deterministic mock models, fake transports, and Ktor MockEngine in tests.
Do not add live provider calls, real provider keys, production prompts, or
customer data to fixtures.
